faqs.question: Can I use AI to handle translating product copy and improving clarity without changing product facts without losing the core detail?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you choose the tool that matches the input type and then review the output yourself. DeepL Write is the best starting point, Grammarly is the strongest second choice, and Copy.ai is the safer fallback when you need a different balance of speed and control.

faqs.question: Which AI tool is better when I need source checking?
faqs.answer: Grammarly is usually the better choice when source checking matters, while DeepL Write is stronger when the job starts with a known set of files or notes.

faqs.question: Is DeepL Write better than Grammarly for this workflow?
faqs.answer: DeepL Write is better when you want the most direct match for the main input; Grammarly is better when the workflow changes enough to justify a different tool.

faqs.question: What should I avoid before I share the result?
faqs.answer: Avoid sharing the first draft until you have checked facts, tone, and any missing context. Use Copy.ai as a helper, not a replacement for review.
